items.title: Carry Traders Shift Away From Dollar for Emerging-Market Bets
items.description: Emerging-market traders are increasingly turning to currencies from the euro to the Australian dollar to fund bets in the developing world as the US dollar roars back.

items.title: Modi’s Biofuel Push Comes Under Pressure as Motorists Protest
items.description: Indian motorists will gather in New Delhi on Sunday, in the first major protest against a flagship biofuel program intended to reduce crude imports and support farm incomes.

items.title: Ebola Crisis Exposes Congo's Fragile Health System
items.description: Daniele Nyirandutiye, Senior Fellow at the Center for American Progress, discusses how the Ebola outbreak in Congo exposed critical weaknesses in healthcare and why long-term investment is essential.

items.title: NZ Pledges Trade Talks With Brazil, Europe Within Five Years
items.description: New Zealand’s governing National Party will begin negotiations for seven new trade deals within five years should it win the November election, Trade Minister Todd McClay said.

items.title: Investors Looking for Shelter From AI Storm Are Turning to India
items.description: After losing out big on the global AI rally, Indian equities are regaining the attention of investors seeking to weather the latest market turbulence.

items.title: Germany’s Second Chance for Growth Rebound Starts Now
items.description: German data in the coming week showing the cumulative impact of the Iran war will set the stage for the government’s latest bid to awaken animal spirits in Europe’s biggest economy.
